pattern: Every node's answer can be derived from comparing how deep its left and right sides reach: whichever side goes deeper carries the deepest nodes, and a tie means this node itself is the meeting point.
hints: For each node, find the depth reached by its left subtree and by its right subtree independently.
If one side reaches strictly deeper than the other, the answer must live entirely within that deeper side.
If both sides reach the exact same depth, this node is the smallest subtree containing all deepest nodes; bubble up (depth+1, this node) as you return from recursion.

edge_inputs.description: several branches reach the max depth; answer is an internal node, not the root
one branch strictly deeper than the other
right-only skewed chain, deepest node is unique so the answer is just that leaf
perfectly balanced tree, both sides tie at every level so the whole tree is the answer
single node at the maximum value bound
left-only skewed chain of depth two, deepest node is unique

stress.description: random general binary tree built by randomly filling open child slots, unique values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 500)))
    vals = rng.sample(range(0, 501), n)
    nodes = {0: [vals[0], None, None]}
    open_slots = [(0, 'left'), (0, 'right')]
    next_id = 1
    for v in vals[1:]:
        idx = rng.randrange(len(open_slots))
        parent_id, side = open_slots.pop(idx)
        nid = next_id
        next_id += 1
        nodes[nid] = [v, None, None]
        if side == 'left':
            nodes[parent_id][1] = nid
        else:
            nodes[parent_id][2] = nid
        open_slots.append((nid, 'left'))
        open_slots.append((nid, 'right'))
    from collections import deque
    out = []
    q = deque([0])
    while q:
        cur = q.popleft()
        if cur is None:
            out.append(None)
            continue
        val, l, r = nodes[cur]
        out.append(val)
        q.append(l)
        q.append(r)
    while out and out[-1] is None:
        out.pop()
    return [out]
